```

<content>
1: # session-audit-series  (seed task: session-audit-001)
2: 
3: ROI (Session self-audit → "The series" + "Output of each pass"): a recurring audit task evaluates the
4: N-2 window of completed sessions, "picks the next un-audited sessions ... never re-auditing one already
5: covered", and each pass emits an audit doc with per-session metrics + an inefficiency ranking by cost
6: saved, plus "A series of concrete fix tasks appended to PLAN.md ... The audit task only diagnoses and
7: enqueues; it does not implement the fixes itself."
8: 
9: This is the shared design doc for the whole `session-audit-NNN` series. Every pass task (`session-audit-001`,
10: `session-audit-002`, …) points here. Depends on **session-metrics-extract** for all numbers.
11: 
12: ## Why
13: The swarm should learn from its own run history and feed concrete fixes back into PLAN — continuously,
14: not once. Diagnosis is the deliverable; implementation is deferred to the enqueued fix tasks so a long
15: analytical pass never blocks on a long code change.
16: 
17: ## Priority-2 lens (ROI: "Mine sessions to simplify the protocol and cut wasted agent work")
18: This series is the mechanism for ROI Priority 2. The bar: anything an agent can be **told once, hinted
19: inline, summarized, or relieved of entirely** should be — every pass should SHRINK each agent's
20: responsibility surface. Scan each transcript through three questions and rank fixes by AGENT-TIME SAVED:
21: - Protocol confusion: agents re-deriving worktree/claim/publish rules, asking where a doc goes,
22:   mishandling submodule-vs-beehive-layer paths, or fighting git plumbing the runner is supposed to own.
23:   Each recurring confusion -> a crisper AGENTS.md rule, an inline prompt hint at the decision point, OR
24:   moving the work out of the agent into the runner.
25: - Time on unrelated tasks: turns spent on setup, exploration, or git mechanics unrelated to the actual
26:   change. Summarize/precompute it and hand the agent the result.
27: - Responsibility removal: if the runner can do something deterministically (path choices, stamping, branch
28:   naming, cleanup), take it OFF the agent's plate.
29: The enqueued fix tasks therefore skew toward prompt/AGENTS.md/runner changes (not only product code), each
30: still shipping its own design doc.
31: 
32: ## Recurrence model (no "recurring" status exists)
33: The series self-perpetuates by enqueuing its successor:
34: 1. When selected, ask `internal/audit` for the next un-audited batch in the N-2 window (two most recent
35:    sessions always excluded — they may be in-flight / under review / in arbitration and would mislead).
36: 2. If the batch is EMPTY: complete as a no-op DONE and do NOT enqueue a successor — the stream rests
37:    until new completed sessions land (a later reconcile or the next manual seed re-arms it). Do not
38:    busy-spin appending empty passes.
39: 3. If non-empty: produce the artifacts below, advance the ledger, and APPEND the successor
40:    `session-audit-<NNN+1>` [TODO] (attempts=0, deps=session-metrics-extract, Doc: this file).
41: Distinct per-pass ids are mandatory so each pass owns a unique audit doc and a unique completion/change
42: doc — never reuse an id.
43: 
44: ## Do (per pass NNN)
45: a. Run `internal/audit` over the N-2 window; get per-session metrics + per-task aggregates + the
46:    trend row for this pass. As of the six current sessions the first window (newest two excluded)
47:    covers bee-bootstrap-1782766865, links-graph-enforcement-1782767318, reconcile-1782772649,
48:    links-graph-enforcement-1782772942; the window advances as new sessions land.
49: b. Write the audit findings doc at EXACTLY `submodules/<sm>/docs/audit/session-audit-<NNN>.md`:
50:    - a per-session metrics table (turns, bytes, kind, taskid, flags);
51:    - findings with session references: why the long sessions are long; reimplementation churn
52:      (links-graph-enforcement ran three times — the canonical waste); lost-race / abandoned sessions
53:      and redundant reconcile loops; completion misses (wrong change-doc path, missing source-branch
54:      push, status left mid-phase); token/context bloat (oversized preambles, re-reading files,
55:      runner-owned git plumbing);
56:    - an inefficiency ranking ordered by estimated cost saved (turns/bytes/retries avoided);
57:    - the tracked metric trend vs prior passes (turns/bytes/retries per delivered task).
58: c. APPEND the concrete fix tasks to PLAN.md, ordered by cost saved, each `## <id> [TODO]
59:    <!-- attempts=0 deps=… weight=… -->` with a body and its OWN design doc under
60:    `submodules/<sm>/docs/tasks/<id>.md`. Tag deps where a fix rides existing code. Diagnose-and-enqueue
61:    ONLY — never implement a fix in this task.
62: d. Advance the audited ledger (mark the covered sessions audited) via `internal/audit` so it stays
63:    deterministic; leave PLAN.md parseable (plan.Parse round-trips).
64: e. Append the successor pass per the recurrence model.
65: f. Write the required completion/change doc at
66:    `submodules/<sm>/docs/bee-session-audit-<NNN>-session-audit-<NNN>.md` (the runner's completion-check
67:    path) carrying the `Beehive: session-audit-<NNN> <doc-path>` stamp; flip session-audit-<NNN> to
68:    NEEDS-REVIEW.
69: 
70: ## Verification
71: Largely analytical, but enforce: ledger advanced (covered sessions now marked audited, none
72: re-audited); every appended fix task has a matching docs/tasks/<id>.md; PLAN.md still parses; the audit
73: doc and the required completion doc both exist at their exact paths. Any helper code this pass adds
74: rides session-metrics-extract's tests.
75: 
76: ## Caveats
77: - Two-most-recent exclusion is non-negotiable; never audit a session that may still be in flight.
78: - The fix tasks are the product; resist implementing them here (a single pass that both diagnoses and
79:   fixes is exactly the long-session waste this workstream exists to kill).
80: - Cost-saved ranking is an estimate — show the inputs (turns/bytes/reruns) so it is auditable, not a
81:   bare number.
82: - Each fix task must ship its own design doc (protocol); a fix enqueued without a doc is incomplete.

(End of file - total 82 lines)
</content>

# AGENTS.md — beehive submodule overlay

Submodule-local rules for honeybees working `submodules/beehive/` (the beehive
product source under `repo/`). Read alongside the repo-root `AGENTS.md` and
`HONEYBEE.md`.

## Sharing modes are an invariant — do not break them

Beehive components converge through git in one of two modes, **detected at
runtime from the repo's remotes with no configuration**: *local sharing* (shared
checkout, keep `main` a clean projection) and *remote sharing* (private checkout,
push/pull; push/pull failures are fatal). A swarm may be hybrid.

Before touching startup, publish, remote, or checkout-health code
(`cmd/honeybee/main.go`, `internal/git/git.go`, `internal/swarm`), read the design
contract:

- **`repo/docs/sharing-modes.md`** — the two modes, the invariants, and the
  startup preflight guard (reset-dirty-with-WARNING; abort-before-tokens if the
  checkout can't be cleaned or the pull target is unreachable).

Do not add a mode flag or config; mode is derived from git remotes. Never author
in the live shared checkout — use a worktree and converge to `main`. A honeybee
`preflight` WARNING/ERROR in the logs is a real defect signal, not noise.

</system-reminder>
```

<content>
1: # session-audit-004 — swarm inefficiency audit (defective-corpus pass; ledger HELD at pass 2)
2: 
3: Series: `session-audit-NNN` (Doc: docs/tasks/session-audit-series.md). Mechanism for ROI **Priority 2** —
4: mine sessions to simplify the protocol and cut wasted agent work — with the ROI **Priority 1** override
5: available (a tokens-per-honeybee lever would enqueue at weight 128). Diagnose-and-enqueue ONLY; the fixes
6: are the two tasks appended to PLAN.md, not implemented here.
7: 
8: ## Headline: the engine window is EMPTY again — and again it is a defect, not a rest
9: 
10: `beehive audit --submodule beehive` (read-only, built from this worktree under the mandated static env)
11: returns an **EMPTY N-2 window**. Per the series rule an empty window means "no-op DONE, do not enqueue a
12: successor — rest." **That rule does not apply here** (my task card's "if unfinalized stubs remain,
13: escalate/re-diagnose — do not rest" branch). The window is empty because of a **new, consumer-side
14: corpus-integrity defect**: the audit header parser rejects every session written after the per-session
15: `model:` stamp landed (commit `248e967`, epoch **1783408030**), so the ten most recent completed sessions
16: — the exact post-`orphan-branch-reclaim-guard` corpus this pass exists to measure — are dropped as
17: "malformed." Completing as a no-op DONE would be a false "caught up" that buries an active, ongoing
18: data-loss bug. This is the same *shape* as session-audit-002's F1 finalization collapse, moved to the
19: **consumer** side and permanent (every future session carries the tag).
20: 
21: ## Pass numbering (task 004 writes NO ledger pass; 005 == ledger pass 3)
22: 
23: session-audit-001 wrote ledger pass 1 (29 rows). session-audit-002 hit a defective corpus and
24: **intentionally wrote nothing** (consumed no ledger pass). session-audit-003 wrote ledger pass 2 (774
25: rows). **This task, session-audit-004, again hits a defective corpus and writes NO ledger pass** (see
26: "Ledger/trend — HELD" below): a `--write` now would fabricate a metric over a corpus that excludes the
27: ten unreadable new sessions. The trend resumes at **session-audit-005 == ledger pass 3**, gated on the
28: parser fix, exactly as 002→003.
29: 
30: ## Corpus census (read-only `beehive audit`, this pass)
31: 
32: | metric | value |
33: |--------|------:|
34: | session files (total) | 914 |
35: | finalized (mineable) | 805 |
36: | **malformed (NEW — unparseable header)** | **10** |
37: | stubs (unfinalized, old residual) | 99 |
38: | mineable fraction | 0.881 |
39: | ledger max audited epoch | 1,783,399,348 |
40: | N-2 engine window | **0 (empty)** |
41: 
42: All 805 finalized sessions are already audited (passes 1–2) or in the N-2 exclusion; the window is empty
43: **only** because the ten sessions that would fill it are unreadable. Every one of the ten has epoch ≥
44: 1,783,408,453 > the ledger's max audited epoch, so all ten are genuinely un-audited — a real, non-empty
45: batch hidden by the parser.
46: 
47: ## F-A — the audit parser rejects the new `model:` header → the series is blind to its most recent (and all future) history → `audit-parse-model-header` (Priority 2, weight 64)
48: 
49: **The dominant finding and it gates the whole workstream** — no later pass can mine what the engine
50: cannot parse.
51: 
52: **Mechanism (producer/consumer schema drift).** Commit `248e967` ("web/stats: stamp per-session model",
53: epoch 1783408030) appended a fourth field to every transcript header; `internal/swarm/swarm.go:824-831`
54: now writes `submodule: <sm> · kind: <kind> · branch: <branch> · model: <model>`. But the audit
55: consumer's header regex (`internal/audit/parse.go:210`) is anchored right after the branch —
56: `…branch:\s*(\S+)\s*$` — so the trailing `· model: …` fails the `$`, `parseHeader` returns
57: `malformed header line "…"`, and `ParseDirCensus` files the session under `Errors` instead of
58: `Sessions`. The mineable corpus silently stops at epoch 1783408030.
59: 
60: **Evidence.**
61: - **10 of 10** finalized sessions with epoch ≥ 1783408030 are rejected as malformed; **zero** post-stamp
62:   sessions parse. (The 11th post-stamp file is this live audit-004 session, correctly excluded as a stub.)
63: - **The hidden batch is real and substantial** — the ten rejected sessions, measured off-disk by the
64:   engine's own PINNED rules (exact `## assistant` lines; `wc -c` bytes; the `model:` field the parser
65:   drops). The two most-recent (†) are N-2-excluded; the other **8 (≈826 turns, ≈2.5 MB)** are the
66:   un-audited batch session-audit-005 will mine once the parser lands:
67: 
68:   | epoch | kind | model | turns | bytes | task |
69:   |------:|------|-------|------:|------:|------|
70:   | 1783408453 | work | opus-4.8 | 85 | 369,218 | session-audit-003 |
71:   | 1783409291 | work | opus-4.8 | 92 | 349,801 | delivery-traceability |
72:   | 1783409945 | review | sonnet-5 | 88 | 181,339 | journal-activity-stream |
73:   | 1783410964 | review | opus-4.8 | 25 | 102,966 | session-audit-003 |
74:   | 1783411148 | work | sonnet-5 | 245 | 676,332 | orphan-branch-reclaim-guard |
75:   | 1783413073 | work | sonnet-5 | 124 | 390,451 | delivery-traceability |
76:   | 1783414572 | review | opus-4.8 | 68 | 207,309 | orphan-branch-reclaim-guard |
77:   | 1783415404 | review | sonnet-5 | 99 | 236,606 | delivery-traceability |
78:   | 1783416244† | work | sonnet-5 | 24 | 103,739 | session-audit-004 (prior GC'd attempt) |
79:   | 1783419846† | review | sonnet-5 | 58 | 217,795 | delivery-traceability |
80: 
81: **Why it is the #1 agent-time saver (Priority-2 lens).** Like F1, it is not a per-session turn cost; it
82: zeroes the mining mechanism for the entire post-stamp corpus and every future session, so
83: session-audit-005+ inherit an empty window and can produce no findings until it lands. It also blocks
84: this pass's own charter — re-measuring the deferred long-turn residual against a clean post-fix baseline
85: is impossible while the post-fix sessions are unreadable. Fixing it is the precondition for every future
86: turn this series would save.
87: 
88: **Priority classification.** Corpus-integrity / consumer-side parse restoration — the same class as the
89: DONE `session-transcript-finalize` — so it takes the **top P2 weight 64**. It is *not itself* a
90: tokens-per-honeybee lever (it changes no injection/preamble/history/working-set/model-routing that a
91: honeybee sees), so it is **not** pulled to Priority 1. Note the downstream P1 value it UNLOCKS: the field
92: it currently discards is the per-session model (the table above already shows an opus/sonnet mix), which
93: is exactly the input the ROI Priority-1 "model routing/caps" analysis needs — hence the fix is scoped to
94: *capture* the model, not merely tolerate it.

96: ## F-B — a malformed-file spike is invisible to the loud alarm (or misattributed to stubs) → `audit-malformed-visibility` (Priority 2, weight 32)
97: 
98: `audit-stub-visibility` (DONE) made an empty window self-explaining — but only for **stubs**.
99: `Census.CorpusBroken` (`internal/audit/aggregate.go:172-177`) `return false` when `len(Stubs)==0` and
100: never consults `ErrorCount()`; `CorpusWarning` (aggregate.go:185-199) names only the stub count and
101: points at `session-transcript-finalize`. So the third census class — malformed files — is invisible to
102: the loud path.
103: 
104: **Observed this pass:** the banner DID fire, but only because the 99 legacy stubs happen to exist, and it
105: blamed the stubs and pointed at the wrong fix (`session-transcript-finalize`) while the actual new defect
106: (the 10 malformed) was named only on a stderr line above the banner. **Had the 99 legacy stubs already
107: been reclaimed, the identical malformed-driven empty window would be byte-for-byte silent** (`CorpusBroken`
108: = false, no stubs) and misread as a healthy rest — precisely the F2 silent-starvation defect, reopened
109: for the malformed class. Fold `ErrorCount()` into `CorpusBroken`/`CorpusWarning` with a distinct
110: "N malformed" line that steers to the parser fix. **Weight 32** — cheap, reuses the existing guard, and
111: ranked below F-A for the same reason F2 ranked below F1: it makes the defect self-announcing rather than
112: restoring the corpus. It is **not** a duplicate of the landed stub guard (that handles `Stubs`;

127: The trend resumes at session-audit-005, run against a parseable corpus after `audit-parse-model-header`.

1: package main

17: // auditCmd runs the deterministic session-metrics extraction and N-2 selection
18: // the session-audit-NNN series consumes. By default it is read-only and prints
19: // machine-readable TSV (the un-audited window, the per-task aggregate, and the
20: // delivered-only trend gauge). With --write it appends the window + trend to the
21: // append-only ledger under submodules/<sm>/docs/audit/, marking those sessions
22: // audited. It reads only the beehive layer (sessions/, PLAN.md, docs/audit/).
23: func auditCmd() *cobra.Command {
24: 	var submodule string
25: 	var write bool
26: 	c := &cobra.Command{
27: 		Use:   "audit",
28: 		Short: "extract reproducible session metrics and select the next un-audited N-2 batch",
29: 		Args:  cobra.NoArgs,
30: 		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
31: 			root, err := findRoot()
32: 			if err != nil {
33: 				return err
34: 			}
35: 			rp, err := repo.Open(root)
36: 			if err != nil {
37: 				return err
38: 			}
39: 			sm, err := resolveSubmodule(rp, submodule)
40: 			if err != nil {
41: 				return err
42: 			}
43: 			// Submodule paths are already absolute (root-joined by Submodules()).
44: 			sessDir := sm.SessionsDir()
45: 			if _, err := os.Stat(sessDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
46: 				fmt.Printf("# no sessions for %s\n", sm.Name)
47: 				return nil
48: 			}
49: 			sessions, census, perr := parseSessions(sessDir)
50: 			if perr != nil {
51: 				return perr
52: 			}
53: 			delivered, err := deliveredSet(sm)
54: 			if err != nil {
55: 				return err
56: 			}
57: 			auditDir := filepath.Join(sm.Path, "docs", "audit")
58: 			led, err := audit.LoadLedger(auditDir)
59: 			if err != nil {
60: 				return err
61: 			}
62: 			pass := led.NextPass()
63: 			window := audit.Window(sessions, led.Audited())
64: 			// Aggregate over the FULL corpus so reruns/retries are true totals; the
65: 			// trend is the delivered-only cost gauge at this pass.
66: 			aggs := audit.Aggregate(sessions, delivered)
67: 			trend := audit.ComputeTrend(aggs, pass)
68: 
69: 			printCensus(census)
70: 			printWindow(window)
71: 			printAggregate(aggs)
72: 			printTrend(trend)
73: 			// Loud, byte-stable corpus-integrity alarm: an empty/sparse window over
74: 			// a corpus of unfinalized stubs is a finalization defect, not a rest.
75: 			// CorpusWarning yields "" (nothing printed) for a healthy corpus.
76: 			if w := census.CorpusWarning(len(window) == 0); w != "" {
77: 				fmt.Fprint(os.Stderr, w)
78: 			}
79: 
80: 			if write {
81: 				led.AppendPass(window, trend)
82: 				if err := led.Save(auditDir); err != nil {
83: 					return err
84: 				}
85: 				fmt.Printf("# wrote pass %d: %d sessions, %d delivered tasks -> %s\n",
86: 					pass, len(window), trend.DeliveredTasks, auditDir)
87: 			}
88: 			return nil
89: 		},
90: 	}
91: 	c.Flags().StringVar(&submodule, "submodule", "", "submodule to audit (default: the only one)")
92: 	c.Flags().BoolVar(&write, "write", false, "append this pass to the docs/audit ledger")
93: 	return c
94: }
95: 
96: // parseSessions runs the corpus census over sessDir and returns the finalized
97: // (mineable) sessions plus the full census (finalized/stub/malformed counts).
98: // Genuinely malformed files are surfaced on stderr (never swallowed) but must not
99: // zero the pass. Unfinalized stubs are NOT errors — they are counted in the
100: // census and drive the corpus-broken warning downstream. A hard error is returned
101: // only when nothing at all is usable or recognisable (no sessions AND no stubs),
102: // or when the directory itself cannot be read.
103: func parseSessions(sessDir string) ([]audit.Session, audit.Census, error) {
104: 	census, err := audit.ParseDirCensus(sessDir)
105: 	if err != nil {
106: 		return nil, audit.Census{}, err
107: 	}
108: 	if len(census.Errors) > 0 {
109: 		joined := errors.Join(census.Errors...)
110: 		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "beehive: audit: skipped %d malformed session(s):\n%v\n", len(census.Errors), joined)
111: 		if census.Finalized() == 0 && census.StubCount() == 0 {
112: 			// Nothing usable and nothing even recognisable as a stub — a real
113: 			// failure, not a rest and not an unfinalized corpus.
114: 			return nil, audit.Census{}, joined
115: 		}
116: 	}
117: 	return census.Sessions, census, nil
118: }
119: 
120: // resolveSubmodule picks the named submodule, or the sole submodule when name is
121: // empty, erroring if the choice is ambiguous or unknown.
122: func resolveSubmodule(rp *repo.Repo, name string) (repo.Submodule, error) {
123: 	subs, err := rp.Submodules()
124: 	if err != nil {
125: 		return repo.Submodule{}, err
126: 	}
127: 	if len(subs) == 0 {
128: 		return repo.Submodule{}, fmt.Errorf("no submodules in repo")
129: 	}
130: 	if name == "" {
131: 		if len(subs) != 1 {
132: 			names := make([]string, len(subs))
133: 			for i, s := range subs {
134: 				names[i] = s.Name
135: 			}
136: 			return repo.Submodule{}, fmt.Errorf("--submodule required (have %s)", strings.Join(names, ", "))
137: 		}
138: 		return subs[0], nil
139: 	}
140: 	for _, s := range subs {
141: 		if s.Name == name {
142: 			return s, nil
143: 		}
144: 	}
145: 	return repo.Submodule{}, fmt.Errorf("unknown submodule %q", name)
146: }
147: 
148: // deliveredSet reads PLAN.md and returns the DONE taskids. A missing PLAN.md
149: // (un-bootstrapped submodule) means nothing delivered yet.
150: func deliveredSet(sm repo.Submodule) (map[string]bool, error) {
151: 	b, err := os.ReadFile(sm.PlanPath())
152: 	if err != nil {
153: 		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
154: 			return map[string]bool{}, nil
155: 		}
156: 		return nil, err
157: 	}
158: 	p, err := plan.Parse(string(b))
159: 	if err != nil {
160: 		return nil, err
161: 	}
162: 	return audit.DeliveredFromPlan(p), nil
163: }
164: 
165: func printCensus(c audit.Census) {
166: 	// The corpus census makes an empty audit window self-explaining:
167: 	// empty-because-audited (a rest) versus empty-because-unfinalized (a defect).
168: 	fmt.Println("# corpus census (finalized=mineable, stub=unfinalized, malformed=broken)")
169: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{"total", "finalized", "stub", "malformed", "mineable_fraction"}, "\t"))
170: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
171: 		strconv.Itoa(c.Total()), strconv.Itoa(c.Finalized()), strconv.Itoa(c.StubCount()),
172: 		strconv.Itoa(c.ErrorCount()), strconv.FormatFloat(c.MineableFraction(), 'f', 3, 64),
173: 	}, "\t"))
174: 	if c.StubCount() > 0 {
175: 		fmt.Println("# unfinalized stubs (sid, branch)")
176: 		fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{"sid", "branch"}, "\t"))
177: 		for _, s := range c.Stubs {
178: 			fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{s.SID, s.Branch}, "\t"))
179: 		}
180: 	}
181: }
182: 
183: func printWindow(w []audit.Session) {
184: 	fmt.Println("# window (N-2, un-audited)")
185: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
186: 		"session_id", "epoch", "kind", "taskid", "bytes", "turns",
187: 		"aborted", "lost_race", "completion_miss", "reconcile_loop",
188: 	}, "\t"))
189: 	for _, s := range w {
190: 		h := s.Heuristics
191: 		fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
192: 			s.ID, strconv.FormatInt(s.Epoch, 10), s.Kind, s.TaskID,
193: 			strconv.FormatInt(s.Bytes, 10), strconv.Itoa(s.Turns),
194: 			strconv.FormatBool(h.Aborted), strconv.FormatBool(h.LostRace),
195: 			strconv.FormatBool(h.CompletionMiss), strconv.FormatBool(h.ReconcileLoop),
196: 		}, "\t"))
197: 	}
198: }
199: 
200: func printAggregate(aggs []audit.TaskAgg) {
201: 	fmt.Println("# per-task aggregate (full corpus)")
202: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{"taskid", "reruns", "retries", "turns", "bytes", "delivered"}, "\t"))
203: 	for _, a := range aggs {
204: 		fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
205: 			a.TaskID, strconv.Itoa(a.Reruns), strconv.Itoa(a.Retries),
206: 			strconv.Itoa(a.Turns), strconv.FormatInt(a.Bytes, 10), strconv.FormatBool(a.Delivered),
207: 		}, "\t"))
208: 	}
209: }
210: 
211: func printTrend(t audit.Trend) {
212: 	fmt.Println("# trend (delivered-only cost gauge)")
213: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
214: 		"pass", "delivered_tasks", "turns", "bytes", "retries",
215: 		"turns_per_task", "bytes_per_task", "retries_per_task",
216: 	}, "\t"))
217: 	fmt.Println(strings.Join([]string{
218: 		strconv.Itoa(t.Pass), strconv.Itoa(t.DeliveredTasks),
219: 		strconv.Itoa(t.Turns), strconv.FormatInt(t.Bytes, 10), strconv.Itoa(t.Retries),
220: 		strconv.FormatFloat(t.TurnsPerTask(), 'f', 1, 64),
221: 		strconv.FormatFloat(t.BytesPerTask(), 'f', 1, 64),
222: 		strconv.FormatFloat(t.RetriesPerTask(), 'f', 2, 64),
223: 	}, "\t"))
224: }

(End of file - total 224 lines)
</content>
```

# LOCALS.md — site facts for this beehive install

Everything specific to THIS machine and this `infra-beehive` install. Generic
behavior is in `AGENTS.md`; the honeybee protocol is in `HONEYBEE.md`. This file is
operator-authored and is NOT managed by `beehive instruction update`.

This install is **self-hosting**: the swarm improves beehive itself. The target
`submodules/beehive/` tracks the beehive source, so a honeybee that lands work on
the `beehive` submodule, once deployed, changes the very binaries running the loop.

## Topology & source of truth

- **`beehive` source** lives at `~/git-repos/spencerharmon/beehive`. Its
  **`origin/main` (GitHub `git@github.com:spencerharmon/beehive.git`) is the single
  source of truth** for the loop's code. Build/deploy always come from `origin/main`.
- **`infra-beehive` (this repo, "the hive") is LOCAL-ONLY: it has NO `origin`.** It
  is the coordination layer. It runs with `receive.denyCurrentBranch=updateInstead`
  so honeybee worktrees publish to the checked-out `main` with no remote. Its
  checked-out `main` is a pure projection of committed history — nothing authors in
  it directly except the operator; honeybees author in their worktrees and publish.
- `submodules/beehive/repo` is a git submodule = a clone of the beehive source.
  Honeybees work tasks in `submodules/beehive/worktrees/<branch>/` and bump the
  gitlink. The publisher self-heals submodule-checkout drift on push (a dirty
  `M repo` after a pointer bump is derived, never authored — it is reset to HEAD).
- GitHub SSH for pushes from the beehive source: `SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/run/user/1000/gcr/ssh`
  (key `~/.ssh/id_ed25519`). No passwordless sudo on this box.
- `/tmp` is quota-limited — write scratch to `~/.cache` (curl `-o`, build tmp, etc.).

## Build & deploy

Deploy script: **`~/.local/bin/beehive-rebuild`** (operator-owned bash). It builds
the three binaries from `beehive` `origin/main` into `~/.local/bin` (temp-then-swap)
and **restarts `beehived` only when its binary changed** (a brief frontend blip; it
never interrupts a honeybee pass). Running it is normal and allowed; do not bounce
`beehived` separately to force-restart in-flight work.

Manual build environment (when building by hand, e.g. to smoke-test):

```
export GOTMPDIR=$HOME/.cache/gotmp TMPDIR=$HOME/.cache/gotmp \
       CGO_ENABLED=0 GOCACHE=$HOME/.cache/go-build
# go1.26.3; module github.com/spencerharmon/beehive
```

Verify an embed/marker landed in a binary: `strings ~/.local/bin/honeybee | grep -c '<marker>'`.

After deploying a protocol/instruction change, run `beehive instruction update` here
to refresh `AGENTS.md`/`HONEYBEE.md`/`BOOTSTRAP.md` to the new defaults (this file is
left untouched).

## Runner config & secret store

Config + gpg keyring live at **`~/.config/beehive/`** (migrated off root-owned
`/etc/beehive` on 2026-07-04; the old `/etc/beehive` tree is left in place but unused). The
binaries resolve the config dir from `BEEHIVE_CONFIG_DIR`, so the systemd units export
`BEEHIVE_CONFIG_DIR=%h/.config/beehive` — both `beehived.service` and the transient honeybee
pass (via `systemd-run --setenv`) — and interactive shells that drive `beehive`/`beehive
secret` must export the same. (Until the binary resolves `~/.config/beehive` user-first on
its own — see the beehive `ROI.md` Configuration item — an unset `BEEHIVE_CONFIG_DIR` falls
back to the stale `/etc/beehive`.)

`~/.config/beehive/config.yaml`: `gpg_home=~/.config/beehive/gnupg`,
`gpg_recipient=beehive@localhost`, `agent_cmd=opencode`,
`model=github-copilot/claude-opus-4.8`, `max_turns=15`, `reject_limit=3`, `ttl_minutes=60`.

**Secrets:** `beehive secret {add,edit,update}` reads/writes `SECRETS.yaml.gpg` (repo-root
global, or per-target with `--submodule <name>`), gpg-encrypted to `beehive@localhost` using
the user keyring under `~/.config/beehive/gnupg` (non-expiring ed25519 key, generated
2026-07-04). `SECRETS.yaml.gpg` is committed (not gitignored); edit it through the worktree
process like any other tracked superproject file. `beehive secret edit` needs `$EDITOR`.

## Scheduling & operation (systemd --user)

- **`beehive-honeybee.timer`** fires `beehive-honeybee.service` on `OnCalendar=*:0/7`
  (every 7 min). A systemd timer won't arm its next trigger while the target unit is
  still active, hence the dense cadence. Each tick launches an independent,
  worktree-isolated honeybee pass via a `systemd-run` transient `run-*.service`.
- **`beehived.service`** runs the frontend:
  `beehived -addr 0.0.0.0:8955 -repo %h/git-repos/spencerharmon/infra-beehive`.
  UI at `http://<host>:8955`. It reads sessions/plans/ROI from this repo.

```

56: | kind: work | 390 |
57: | kind: review | 372 |
58: | kind: arbitrate | 8 |
59: | kind: reconcile | 4 |
60: | flag: aborted | 207 |
61: | flag: completion_miss (work+aborted) | 196 |
62: | flag: lost_race | 1 |
63: | flag: reconcile_loop | 0 |
64: 
65: Abort reasons (from the ledger `abort_reason` column, pass-2 rows):
66: 
67: | abort reason | count |
68: |--------------|------:|
69: | per-turn timeout — `turn N exceeded … per-turn timeout (stalled agent); abandoning for GC` | 198 |
70: | idle timeout — `turn N made no progress within the 15m0s idle timeout (stalled agent)` | 8 |
71: | `lost the claim race … reselecting` | 1 |
72: 
73: **The critical split is chronological, not categorical.** Anchoring the window against the landed
74: question-tool disable (commit `78bd99e`, epoch **1783306971**) partitions the aborts into a fixed
75: *past* and a live *present*:
76: 
77: | segment | sessions | aborts | dominant abort mode |
78: |---------|---------:|-------:|---------------------|
79: | pre-fix (epoch < 1783306971) | 752 | 196 | per-turn timeout (interactive elicitation) |
80: | **post-fix (epoch ≥ 1783306971)** | **22** | **11** | **idle timeout (doomed review)** |
81: 
82: Every one of the 198 per-turn stalls is pre-fix; every one of the 8 idle-timeout stalls is post-fix.
83: The abort mode *moved*. That partition drives the three findings below: the pre-fix mode is fixed
84: (F-FIXED-1), the review-churn it fed is fixed (F-FIXED-2), and the post-fix idle mode is the one live
85: sink (F-LIVE).
86: 
87: ## F-FIXED-1 — interactive-elicitation per-turn stalls: FIXED by `78bd99e`, NOT enqueued
88: 
89: **This was the single largest historical sink and it is already closed.** Of the 198 per-turn-timeout
90: aborts, 171 (86%) are the classic headless-elicitation stall: a honeybee calls an interactive
91: question/ask tool, has no channel to answer, and blocks the whole runner-turn until the 1h GC —
92: discarding every turn it produced first.
93: 
94: - **171 sessions / 29 distinct tasks / ~15,851 discarded turns (30% of the window) / ~85 MB.**
95: - **Epoch range 1782854469 … 1783304907 — 100% before the fix.**
96: 
97: The fix already landed: **`78bd99e` (epoch 1783306971)** sets `"tools": {"question": false}` in
98: `internal/swarm/opencode.go` (line ~216), removing the interactive tool from the headless agent's
99: surface — exactly the "remove the footgun at the runner" lever, shipped. **Validation against the
100: finalized corpus: zero interactive-elicitation per-turn stalls after 1783306971** (post-fix per-turn
101: timeouts = 3, and none is an elicitation call). Enqueuing an `honeybee-elicitation-guard` now would
102: re-implement a DONE fix — so it is **not enqueued**. HONEYBEE.md absolute-rule #2 (the prohibition)
103: plus the tool-surface removal together hold; the data confirms it.
104: 
105: ## F-FIXED-2 — redundant re-review churn: mitigated by landed publish/dispatch guards, NOT enqueued
106: 
107: The pre-fix corpus also shows heavy re-review: 372 review passes vs. 390 work passes, with individual
108: tasks re-reviewed dozens of times, **every verdict an APPROVE** (editor-safety-guards 61,
109: hive-hygiene-panel 51, dashboard-cards 44, multi-repo-config-migration 42, editor-session-persist 42).
110: The mechanism is redundant approval: an approved task did not promptly leave `NEEDS-REVIEW` on `main`,
111: so the runner kept re-selecting it.
112: 
113: But this churn **stopped ~epoch 1783114** — coincident with the landing of the guards that target
114: exactly it: **`publish-advance-guard` (DONE, epoch 1783091303)** judges completion on the origin stamp
115: (an approval that lands cannot loop back), and **`reconcile-dedup-guard` (DONE)** established the
116: dispatch-time re-pull/short-circuit pattern; the publish conflict-resolution cluster
117: (`63ae784`/`92472d7`, ~1783107–1783108) hardened the approval-merge path. **Validation:** after the
118: `gc-source-branch-reclaim` boundary (epoch 1783114508), no task shows the dozens-of-approvals
119: signature; the only tasks reviewed >1× are (a) the orphan-loop victims below and (b) a handful of
120: 2–3-count *legitimate change-request cycles*. So a `review-dispatch-dedup` task would duplicate landed
121: work — **not enqueued**. The *residual* review waste is a different mechanism (F-LIVE).
122: 
123: ## F-LIVE — a NEEDS-REVIEW task whose implementer work is unreachable spawns doomed review passes that idle-time-out forever → `orphan-branch-reclaim-guard` (Priority 2, weight 64)
124: 
125: **The one sink still burning agent-time today.** All 8 idle-timeout aborts are post-fix, and **6 of
126: the 8 are review passes against just two tasks** whose reviewable commit does not exist in the shared
127: object store. This is not elicitation (the tool is gone) and not redundant-approval churn (there is no
128: approval — there is nothing to approve): it is a **doomed-review loop**.
129: 
130: **Mechanism (read directly from the transcripts).**
131: 1. A WORK pass finishes and tries to push `bee-<task>` to the submodule origin, but `origin/bee-<task>`
132:    holds a **divergent orphan from a prior GC'd attempt** → the push is rejected non-fast-forward. Per
133:    HONEYBEE.md (no force-push, no origin reconcile) the work is left on the local branch "for the
134:    runner to reclaim," and the task is flipped `NEEDS-REVIEW`.
135: 2. That local branch/commit is then GC'd with the worktree. `gc-source-branch-reclaim` (DONE) reclaims
136:    only *merged* branches; this branch could never merge (non-fast-forward), so it is **not reclaimed —
137:    it is lost**. The task now sits `NEEDS-REVIEW` pointing at a commit reachable **nowhere**.
138: 3. The runner selects it and spawns a review pass. The review prompt says "fetch from origin if the
139:    branch is absent locally," but in a **shared-checkout worktree `submodules/beehive/repo` has no
140:    `origin` remote** (`fatal: 'origin' does not appear to be a git repository`), so the documented
141:    fallback cannot even run. The reviewer spelunks git internals (`.git/modules/…`, worktree lists,
142:    object stores), finds nothing, and **idle-times-out**. The task stays `NEEDS-REVIEW` → re-dispatched
143:    next cycle → **loop, ~15 min + a full session's tokens burned each time, indefinitely, invisibly.**
144: 
145: **Evidence (window):**
146: - **`journal-activity-stream` — STILL STUCK RIGHT NOW.** `NEEDS-REVIEW` at claim
147:   `session=beehive-1783404445-4154487` (heartbeat 2026-07-07T06:38). Its change doc claims pointer
148:   `053580d`; `git --git-dir=<shared module> cat-file -t 053580d` ⇒ *Not a valid object name*;
149:   `origin/bee-journal-activity-stream` ⇒ `fb3e3cfa` (the dead orphan). **4 review passes idle-timed-out**
150:   (`…384949`, `…389368`, `…392985`, `…398925`), each independently re-discovering the work is gone.
151: - **`session-transcript-finalize` — same mechanism.** A reviewer recorded "the claimed reviewable
152:   commit `9a9e071` exists NOWHERE (not in the submodule object store, no reflog)"; the `git fetch
153:   origin bee-session-transcript-finalize` fallback returned `fatal: 'origin' does not appear to be a
154:   git repository`. 2 review passes + 1 work pass idle-timed-out here before the task eventually landed.
155: 
156: **Why this is a runner fix (the P2 lens: stop agents fighting git plumbing the runner should own).**
157: The reviewer cannot be told its way out — the reviewable commit is *physically absent* and the
158: prescribed recovery command (`fetch origin`) is inapplicable in shared-checkout mode. The runner must
159: own two deterministic guards (detailed in the design doc):
160: 1. **Publish-side (don't strand work):** when a work-pass source-branch push is rejected
161:    non-fast-forward because `origin/bee-<task>` is a dead orphan, reconcile it deterministically
162:    (`fetch` + `merge -s ours` the dead orphan, then push — the recovery the reviewer itself named) so
163:    the new work lands; if it truly cannot land, leave the task **workable** (TODO/NEEDS-HUMAN with the
164:    concrete reason), never `NEEDS-REVIEW` at an unreachable commit.
165: 2. **Review-dispatch-side (don't spawn doomed passes):** before spawning a review, deterministically
166:    verify the reviewable commit is reachable in the shared module store; if not, bounce the task to
167:    `NEEDS-ARBITRATION` (or TODO for re-work) with a concrete "reviewable commit unreachable" reason
168:    instead of a 15-minute idle stall — and fix the `fetch origin` fallback to target the shared module
169:    store in shared-checkout mode.
170: 
171: **Priority-2 classification.** Responsibility-removal / runner-owned git plumbing (ROI Priority 2,
172: "cut wasted agent work") — same class as the DONE `reconcile-dedup-guard` and `gc-source-branch-reclaim`,
173: whose gap it closes. It eliminates whole wasted *sessions* and unblocks permanently-stuck delivered
174: tasks, so it takes the **top P2 weight 64** this pass. It is not a token-*content* lever (it does not
175: trim injection/preamble/history/working-set/model-routing), so it is **not** pulled to Priority 1 — and
176: this pass fabricates no P1 promotion, because the one P1 tool-surface lever the raw data suggested
177: (`question`-tool removal) is already DONE (F-FIXED-1).
178: 
179: ## Considered, NOT enqueued
180: - **F-FIXED-1 `honeybee-elicitation-guard` and F-FIXED-2 `review-dispatch-dedup`** — the two biggest
181:   raw sinks, both closed by landed commits (above). Re-enqueuing either is the series' cardinal sin.
182: - **The 2 non-review idle-timeouts + 3 post-fix per-turn timeouts (5 sessions).** One work pass on
183:   `session-transcript-finalize` and one on `session-audit-003` (a prior GC'd attempt of *this* task)
184:   idle-stalled on long analysis, not the orphan loop; 3 post-fix per-turn timeouts are long single
185:   turns (subagent/`task` spawns eating the parent clock). Real but small (5 sessions) and riskier to
186:   fix (turn/commit semantics). **Deferred to session-audit-004** to re-measure once F-LIVE removes the
187:   6 doomed-review idle stalls and leaves a clean residual.
188: - **The 27 pre-fix non-interactive per-turn stalls** — pre-fix history; re-measure post-fix rather than
189:   enqueue against a corpus that no longer generates them.
190: 
191: ## Tracked metric trend (delivered-only cost gauge; resumes here)
192: 
193: | pass | delivered_tasks | turns | bytes | retries | turns/task | bytes/task | retries/task |
194: |-----:|----------------:|------:|------:|--------:|-----------:|-----------:|-------------:|
195: | 1 | 8 | 1,044 | 3,909,320 | 12 | 130.5 | 488,665.0 | 1.50 |
196: | 2 | 47 | 52,378 | 260,693,020 | 729 | 1,114.4 | 5,546,660.0 | 15.51 |
197: 
198: The trend gauges the whole finalized corpus's delivered-task cost at each pass. Pass 2 is the **first
199: honest full-corpus baseline post-finalization**: the 130.5 → 1,114.4 turns/task and 1.50 → 15.51
200: retries/task jump is **newly-visible history** (pass 1 measured an 8-task sliver; pass 2 measured the
201: whole minable corpus), not a live regression — most of it is the pre-fix elicitation-stall churn
202: (F-FIXED-1) plus its re-dispatch/re-review tail (F-FIXED-2), both now closed. The live residual driving
203: future retries is F-LIVE (the doomed-review loop); the enqueued fix targets it directly. Future passes
204: over incremental post-fix windows will track the real per-window trend against this baseline.
205:

> 💭 I see the distinction now—the existing guard in `bounceIfUnreachable` catches when a commit is unreachable and dead, routing it to arbitration. But what I'm looking at is the inverse scenario: the commit has already made it into the submodule's main branch on origin, yet the hive-side DONE status hasn't caught up yet.
> 
> In the delivery-traceability case, review #1 successfully merged the branch and pushed it, but the hive commit that would bump the gitlink and flip DONE never landed. So when the runner re-selects the task based on the stale PLAN.md, `bounceIfUnreachable` sees the old pointer is still reachable (it's an ancestor of origin/main) and doesn't bounce—it spawns a full review #2 that discovers the branch is already merged. The gap is that there's no symmetric check for "branch already merged into tracked main"—the runner could skip the review entirely and deterministically finalize DONE by bumping the gitlink and flipping the status.
> 
> This is a real, actionable finding in the same class as the orphan-branch guard, and it would save a full review cycle. But I'm second-guessing whether it's worth prioritizing: the condition requires review #1's hive-layer commit to fail after the submodule push succeeds—a non-atomic hazard that can happen if the review is interrupted between the external push and the local merge. It's structural and can recur, but in this window it happened once, so it's a tail event.
> 
> The fix is cheap—just a pre-dispatch check reusing existing merge-base plumbing—so I'm weighing it at 32, same tier as the audit-malformed-visibility guard. It's structurally similar to orphan-branch-reclaim-guard (which was 64), but that one eliminated six sessions while this eliminates one, so the lower weight is honest.
> 
> Now I need to check if this qualifies as a P1 tokens-per-honeybee lever.  It's a dispatch guard that prevents spawning a redundant review session entirely, which saves all that session's tokens—but the actual lever is "don't dispatch a doomed pass," which is runner responsibility-removal, not a token-content optimization like injection/preamble/history/model-routing. So it stays P2 by the same logic that kept orphan-branch-reclaim-guard and audit-malformed-visibility at P2: they eliminate sessions but aren't token-content levers.

cmd/beehive/cmd_submodule.go:17:func findRoot() (string, error) {
--- findRoot body ---
func findRoot() (string, error) {
	d, err := os.Getwd()
	if err != nil {
		return "", err
	}
	for {
		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(d, repo.AgentsFile)); err == nil {
			return d, nil
		}
		p := filepath.Dir(d)
		if p == d {
			return "", fmt.Errorf("not inside a beehive repo (no %s found)", repo.AgentsFile)
		}
		d = p
	}
}

```

1: # orphan-branch-reclaim-guard
2: 
3: ROI (Priority 2 "cut wasted agent work"; and Deferred features → "Pushed source-branch reclamation"
4: whose gap this closes; and journal-activity-stream's ROI line "the race-safe guarantees are currently
5: no-ops; coordination must actually work"): a task set `NEEDS-REVIEW` whose implementer commit is
6: unreachable must never spawn review passes that idle-time-out forever.
7: 
8: ## Why
9: A work pass finishes and pushes `bee-<taskid>` to the submodule origin, but `origin/bee-<taskid>`
10: already holds a **divergent orphan from a prior GC'd attempt**, so the push is rejected
11: non-fast-forward. Per HONEYBEE.md (no force-push, no origin reconcile) the work is left on the local
12: branch "for the runner to reclaim," and the task is flipped `NEEDS-REVIEW`. Then the worktree is GC'd:
13: `gc-source-branch-reclaim` reclaims only *merged* branches, so this never-merged branch is dropped and
14: its commit becomes reachable **nowhere**. The task now sits `NEEDS-REVIEW` pointing at a phantom commit.
15: 
16: The runner keeps selecting it and spawning review passes. The review prompt says "fetch from origin if
17: the branch is absent locally," but in a **shared-checkout worktree `submodules/<sm>/repo` has no
18: `origin` remote** (`fatal: 'origin' does not appear to be a git repository`), so the fallback cannot
19: run. Each reviewer spelunks git internals, finds nothing, and idle-times-out (15m) — then the task
20: stays `NEEDS-REVIEW` and is re-dispatched. Observed live: `journal-activity-stream` (commit `053580d`
21: absent; `origin` = orphan `fb3e3cfa`; 4 review passes idle-timed-out; still stuck) and
22: `session-transcript-finalize` (commit `9a9e071`/`e5d00d7` "exists NOWHERE"; 2 reviews + 1 work
23: idle-timed-out). Every cycle burns a full session; the delivered task never completes and blocks its
24: dependents.
25: 
26: ## Do
27: Two deterministic, runner-owned guards (rides `internal/swarm` publish/GC/dispatch + `internal/git`;
28: neighbours the DONE `gc-source-branch-reclaim` / `publish-advance-guard`):
29: 
30: 1. **Publish-side — don't strand work.** When a work-pass `bee-<taskid>` push is rejected
31:    non-fast-forward because `origin/bee-<taskid>` is a *dead orphan* (a ref from an earlier attempt of
32:    the same task with no shared ancestry to the new commit), reconcile it deterministically —
33:    `git fetch origin bee-<taskid>` then `git merge -s ours origin/bee-<taskid>` to supersede the dead
34:    orphan, then push (no force-push; ours-merge preserves the orphan's ancestry so the push
35:    fast-forwards). Only if the work still cannot be made to land, leave the task **workable**
36:    (`TODO` for re-work, or `NEEDS-HUMAN` with the concrete reason) — **never** `NEEDS-REVIEW` at a
37:    commit the reviewer cannot reach.
38: 2. **Review-dispatch-side — don't spawn doomed passes.** Before spawning a review pass, deterministically
39:    verify the task's reviewable commit (its recorded submodule-pointer bump / `bee-<taskid>` tip) is
40:    reachable in the shared module object store. If it is NOT (and no in-flight work-pass on any host
41:    still holds it — distinguish *lost* from *not-yet-pushed*), do not spawn the review: bounce the task
42:    to `NEEDS-ARBITRATION` (or `TODO` for re-work) with a concrete `reviewable commit <sha> unreachable`
43:    reason. Never let a review pass idle-stall on a phantom commit.
44: 3. **Fix the shared-checkout fetch fallback.** In shared (local) mode `submodules/<sm>/repo` has no
45:    `origin`; the reviewer's "fetch the branch" recovery must resolve against the **shared module store**
46:    (`.git/modules/…`) rather than a nonexistent per-worktree origin, so a reachable-but-not-local commit
47:    is still reviewable. Update the review prompt/runner path accordingly.
48: 
49: ## Tests (swarm_test.go / git_test.go)
50: - Origin carries a divergent-orphan `bee-x` from a prior attempt; a new work commit is pushed → assert
51:   the runner reconciles (ours-merge) and the new commit lands reachable; task ends `NEEDS-REVIEW` at a
52:   reachable commit (regression: without the guard the push is rejected and the commit is lost).
53: - Task `NEEDS-REVIEW` whose recorded commit is absent from the module store → assert dispatch does NOT
54:   spawn a review pass and instead sets `NEEDS-ARBITRATION` with the "unreachable" reason (regression:
55:   before the change a doomed pass is spawned and idle-times-out).
56: - Shared-checkout mode (submodule repo has no `origin`), commit present only in the module store →
57:   assert the reviewer can resolve it (fallback targets the module store, not `origin`).
58: - Happy path: reachable commit, normal `origin` → review dispatches unchanged (no regression).
59: 
60: ## Caveats
61: - No force-push and no destructive origin rewrite (HONEYBEE.md invariant); reconcile only via
62:   `merge -s ours`, which keeps the dead orphan reachable as an ancestor.
63: - Must distinguish a **lost** commit (GC'd, re-work needed) from one **not yet pushed** by an in-flight
64:   peer work-pass — only bounce when no live claim/work-pass can still land it, or a legitimate race is
65:   mis-flagged as unreachable.
66: - Shares the publish/GC/dispatch path with `gc-source-branch-reclaim`, `publish-advance-guard`,
67:   `worktree-sync-base`; keep hunks separate and rebase on tip.
68: - Honour both sharing modes (`repo/docs/sharing-modes.md`): remote mode fetches from `origin`; local
69:   mode resolves against the shared module store. Do not add a mode flag — derive from remotes.

(End of file - total 69 lines)
</content>
```

Instructions from: /home/spencer/git-repos/spencerharmon/infra-beehive/.worktrees/beehive-1783429202-734855/submodules/beehive/AGENTS.md
# AGENTS.md — beehive submodule overlay

Submodule-local rules for honeybees working `submodules/beehive/` (the beehive
product source under `repo/`). Read alongside the repo-root `AGENTS.md` and
`HONEYBEE.md`.

## Sharing modes are an invariant — do not break them

Beehive components converge through git in one of two modes, **detected at
runtime from the repo's remotes with no configuration**: *local sharing* (shared
checkout, keep `main` a clean projection) and *remote sharing* (private checkout,
push/pull; push/pull failures are fatal). A swarm may be hybrid.

Before touching startup, publish, remote, or checkout-health code
(`cmd/honeybee/main.go`, `internal/git/git.go`, `internal/swarm`), read the design
contract:

- **`repo/docs/sharing-modes.md`** — the two modes, the invariants, and the
  startup preflight guard (reset-dirty-with-WARNING; abort-before-tokens if the
  checkout can't be cleaned or the pull target is unreachable).

Do not add a mode flag or config; mode is derived from git remotes. Never author
in the live shared checkout — use a worktree and converge to `main`. A honeybee
`preflight` WARNING/ERROR in the logs is a real defect signal, not noise.

</system-reminder>

113: session-audit-004 itself* (5m idle, completion_miss). The whole remaining long-turn tail is now **the
114: audit series' own analysis pass self-stalling** (heavy transcript reads + a Go build that hit `/tmp`
115: disk-quota in the sibling `session-audit-003` attempt, forcing a manual `GOTMPDIR` redirect). It is one
116: data point, largely *transitional* build friction (gone once the fix is deployed and `beehive audit`
117: just runs) and *inherent* analysis cost the deterministic engine already offloads for the quantitative
118: half. Enqueuing a turn/build fix on n=1 would fabricate against session-audit-002's discipline —
119: **re-measured, not enqueued** (see below).
120: 
121: ## F-1 — a review whose submodule merge landed but whose hive DONE-flip didn't spawns a full redundant re-review → `review-already-merged-guard` (Priority 2, weight 32)
122: 
123: **The one live runner-gap this pass found.** A review's two effects are **not atomic**: it (1) merges
124: `bee-<task>` into the submodule's tracked `main` and **pushes to origin** (irreversible), then (2) commits
125: the hive-layer gitlink bump + PLAN `NEEDS-REVIEW → DONE` for the runner to land. When a review is
126: interrupted between them, the code is **merged-and-DONE at origin** while the hive PLAN still reads
127: `NEEDS-REVIEW` at a pre-merge pointer — so the runner re-selects the task and spawns a **whole second
128: review** to re-discover the merge.
129: 
130: **Evidence (window):** `delivery-traceability` review #12 (`…1783419846`, **58 turns, ~218 KB**, sonnet-5).
131: Its own note: *"the implementer's branch was already merged into the submodule's tracked main and pushed
132: to origin as `a54123c` — left mid-flight by a prior interrupted review attempt (PLAN.md was still
133: NEEDS-REVIEW, pointer unbumped)."* The prior review #10 had merged+pushed `a02a886..a54123c` (post-orphan-
134: fix) but its hive DONE-flip never landed; #12 re-fetched, re-tested, re-diffed to byte-identity, and only
135: then completed the DONE — a full session spent on bookkeeping.
136: 
137: **Why the existing guard misses it.** `orphan-branch-reclaim-guard`'s review-dispatch guard
138: (`bounceIfUnreachable`, `internal/swarm/swarm.go:1830`) handles the **opposite** case — reviewable commit
139: reachable *nowhere* → `NEEDS-ARBITRATION`. Here the recorded pointer **is** reachable (it is an ancestor
140: of tracked `main`), so `bounceIfUnreachable` correctly does not fire — and a redundant review is
141: dispatched. The **symmetric** case (already merged into tracked main) has no handler.
142: 
143: **The fix (diagnose-and-enqueue; docs/tasks/review-already-merged-guard.md).** Before spawning a review,
144: deterministically check whether `bee-<task>` is already an **ancestor of the submodule's tracked `main`**.
145: If so, the work was already approved-and-landed (submodule `main` advances only via approved-review
146: merges — ancestry ⟺ prior approval, so auto-finalize is safe): the runner finalizes DONE (bump gitlink to
147: the merge, flip PLAN, unlock dependents) **without a review pass**. Reuses the ancestor/merge-base
148: plumbing next to `CommitReachable`; the three dispatch outcomes become exhaustive and disjoint
149: (merged→DONE / unmerged-reachable→review / unreachable→ARBITRATION).
150: 
151: **Priority-2 classification.** Responsibility-removal / runner-owned git plumbing (ROI Priority 2, "cut
152: wasted agent work") — the same class as `orphan-branch-reclaim-guard`, whose dispatch guard it completes.
153: It eliminates a whole redundant review *session* per occurrence (~58 turns / ~218 KB observed). It is
154: **not** a token-*content* lever (it trims no injection/preamble/history/working-set/model-routing that a
155: honeybee sees), so it is **not** pulled to Priority 1 — identical reasoning to why
156: `orphan-branch-reclaim-guard` and `audit-malformed-visibility` stayed P2. **Weight 32** (not 64): a cheap
157: guard reusing existing plumbing, but a *tail* trigger (1 occurrence here) versus the 6-session live loop
158: that earned orphan-branch-reclaim-guard its 64.
159: 
160: ## Considered, NOT enqueued
161: 
162: - **The single post-fix idle stall (row 11, the audit self-stall).** n=1, transitional build friction +
163:   inherent analysis cost; the deterministic `beehive audit` already carries the quantitative load. A
164:   turn/build fix on one data point repeats session-audit-002's refusal to fabricate. Re-measured above,
165:   not enqueued.
166: - **A tokens-per-honeybee (Priority-1, weight 128) pull-forward.** All four P1 levers are already **DONE**:
167:   `honeybee-injection-trim`, `honeybee-task-brief`, `honeybee-turn-context-diff`, `honeybee-model-routing`
168:   (each weight 128). The observed opus/sonnet mix is **operator config churn during the window, not a
169:   routing defect** — routing is per-kind, opt-in, and landed (`swarm.go:772-792` `ModelFor`); the data
170:   shows no clean per-kind split because the config was being tuned, not because routing is broken.
171:   Re-enqueuing any would be the series' cardinal sin. **No P1 lever surfaced; none fabricated.**
172: - **The 245-turn `orphan-branch-reclaim-guard` work (row 7) and the 407 KB reconcile (row 2).** The 245
173:   turns are genuine complexity (implementing the swarm/git publish + conflict-reconciliation code; its
174:   merge/conflict mentions are topical to the change, with almost no re-reading signal), not waste. The
175:   large reconcile is a pre-stamp one-off with no recurring pattern. Neither is a fix.
176: - **F-FIXED-1 `honeybee-elicitation-guard` / F-FIXED-2 `review-dispatch-dedup`** — both closed by landed
177:   commits and re-validated above; re-enqueuing either is the cardinal sin.

```
<path>/home/spencer/git-repos/spencerharmon/infra-beehive/.worktrees/beehive-1783429202-734855/submodules/beehive/docs/tasks/review-already-merged-guard.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # review-already-merged-guard
2: 
3: ROI (Priority 2 "cut wasted agent work" — runner-owned coordination that must actually work): a task
4: still `NEEDS-REVIEW` in the hive PLAN whose implementer branch is **already merged into the submodule's
5: tracked `origin/main`** must never spawn a fresh review pass — the runner must deterministically finalize
6: it DONE instead of paying a full re-review session to re-discover the merge.
7: 
8: This is the missing **symmetric half** of the DONE `orphan-branch-reclaim-guard` review-dispatch guard.
9: That guard (`bounceIfUnreachable`, `internal/swarm/swarm.go:1830`) handles the *commit-LOST* case (the
10: recorded reviewable pointer is reachable **nowhere** → bounce to `NEEDS-ARBITRATION`). It has no handler
11: for the opposite, equally-real case: the reviewable commit is reachable **and already landed on tracked
12: main** — approval happened, only the hive-side bookkeeping (gitlink bump + PLAN `NEEDS-REVIEW → DONE`)
13: didn't. In that case the recorded pointer IS reachable, so `bounceIfUnreachable` (correctly) does not
14: fire, and a doomed-*redundant* review is dispatched.
15: 
16: ## Why
17: The submodule-origin merge and the hive-layer DONE bookkeeping a review performs are **not atomic**:
18: 
19: 1. An approving review runs `git merge bee-<taskid>` into the submodule's tracked `main` and **pushes it
20:    to origin** (an irreversible external side-effect) — e.g. `origin/main` advances to the merge commit.
21: 2. It then commits the hive-layer change in its own worktree (bump the `submodules/<sm>/repo` gitlink to
22:    that merge + flip PLAN `NEEDS-REVIEW → DONE` + unlock dependents) for the runner to merge to hive
23:    `main`.
24: 3. If the review is interrupted between (1) and the runner landing (2) — pass GC/timeout, or a hive-`main`
25:    PLAN merge that doesn't complete — the code is **merged-and-DONE at the origin level** while the hive
26:    PLAN still reads `NEEDS-REVIEW` with the gitlink pointing pre-merge.
27: 4. The runner re-selects the task (still `NEEDS-REVIEW` in the hive PLAN) and, because its recorded
28:    pointer is perfectly reachable, spawns a **full review pass** that re-fetches, re-tests, re-diffs, and
29:    only then discovers "already merged, nothing to do" and completes the DONE flip — a whole session
30:    burned on bookkeeping.
31: 
32: **Observed live (session-audit-005 window, ledger pass 3):** `delivery-traceability` review pass
33: `bee-delivery-traceability-1783419846-483470` (58 turns, ~218 KB, sonnet-5). Its own note: *"the
34: implementer's branch was already merged into the submodule's tracked main and pushed to origin as
35: `a54123c` — left mid-flight by a prior interrupted review attempt (PLAN.md was still NEEDS-REVIEW,
36: pointer unbumped)."* The prior review `…1783415404` (pass 10 in-window) had merged+pushed
37: `a02a886..a54123c` post-orphan-fix but its hive DONE-flip never landed; the re-review re-did the whole
38: judgement to reach the same DONE. One redundant session per occurrence; a structural (not fluke) hazard
39: of the non-atomic split that recurs whenever a review is interrupted after the origin push.
40: 
41: ## Do
42: One deterministic, runner-owned review-dispatch guard, mirroring `bounceIfUnreachable` (same call site in
43: `internal/swarm/swarm.go` where a review pass is about to be spawned; reuses `internal/git`
44: ancestor/merge-base plumbing next to `CommitReachable`):
45: 
46: Before spawning a review pass for a `NEEDS-REVIEW` task, check whether the implementer's `bee-<taskid>`
47: tip (equivalently its recorded submodule-pointer commit) is **already an ancestor of the submodule's
48: tracked `main`** (origin `main` in remote mode; the shared module store's `main` in local mode — derive
49: from remotes exactly as the orphan guard does, no mode flag). If it IS already merged:
50: - Do **not** spawn a review. The work was already approved-and-landed (submodule `main` only advances via
51:   approved-review merges, so ancestry-of-main ⟺ a prior approval merged it — auto-finalizing is safe, it
52:   merely completes the interrupted bookkeeping).
53: - Deterministically finalize: bump the hive `submodules/<sm>/repo` gitlink to the merge commit, flip the
54:   PLAN task `NEEDS-REVIEW → DONE`, unlock dependents, and record a terse "already merged into tracked
55:   main by a prior interrupted review; runner-finalized DONE (no re-review)" note. Commit + publish via the
56:   same path the orphan guard's `BounceUnreachable` uses.
57: 
58: If the tip is NOT an ancestor of tracked main → fall through to the existing dispatch (the normal review,
59: or `bounceIfUnreachable` when the commit is unreachable). The three review-dispatch outcomes are then
60: exhaustive and disjoint: reachable-and-merged → finalize DONE (this guard); reachable-not-merged → review
61: (unchanged); unreachable → `NEEDS-ARBITRATION` (`orphan-branch-reclaim-guard`).
62: 
63: ## Tests (swarm_test.go / git_test.go)
64: - Task `NEEDS-REVIEW`; `bee-x` already merged into the submodule's tracked `main` (ancestor of `main`),
65:   hive PLAN still `NEEDS-REVIEW` + gitlink pre-merge → assert dispatch does **not** spawn a review, and
66:   the runner flips the task `DONE`, bumps the gitlink to the merge, and unlocks dependents (regression:
67:   before the guard a full redundant review pass is spawned).
68: - Reachable-but-NOT-an-ancestor commit (genuine pending work) → assert a normal review is dispatched
69:   (no false-positive auto-DONE, no regression).
70: - Unreachable commit → assert the existing `orphan-branch-reclaim-guard` bounce to `NEEDS-ARBITRATION`
71:   still fires (the two guards do not shadow each other).
72: - Local (shared-checkout, no `origin`) and remote modes both resolve ancestry against the correct store.
73: - Dependents `deps=<task>` unlock on the runner-finalized DONE exactly as on a review-driven DONE.
74: 
75: ## Caveats
76: - Auto-finalize **only** on true ancestry of tracked `main` (a real merge preserves the commit as an
77:   ancestor); never on a mere name/branch match or a reachable-but-unmerged tip — that would mark unreviewed
78:   work DONE. When in doubt, fall through to a normal review (safe default).
79: - Distinguish *already-merged* (ancestor of main → finalize) from *not-yet-pushed by an in-flight peer*
80:   (reachable via a live work claim but not yet on main → normal review), just as the orphan guard
81:   distinguishes *lost* from *not-yet-pushed*.
82: - Shares the review-dispatch/publish path with `orphan-branch-reclaim-guard`, `publish-advance-guard`,
83:   `gc-source-branch-reclaim`; keep the hunk separate and rebase on tip. Honour both sharing modes
84:   (`repo/docs/sharing-modes.md`); derive mode from remotes, do not add a flag.
85: - Reporting/dispatch only — does not change what a review judges when one IS spawned.

(End of file - total 85 lines)
</content>
```

7: ## What
8: Pass of the recurring session self-audit series (ROI Priority 2 — mine sessions to simplify the protocol
9: and cut wasted agent work). Ran `internal/audit` over the beehive corpus; the N-2 engine window is
10: **EMPTY — but again because of a corpus-integrity defect, not a rested swarm** (my task card's "if

16: ## Headline finding
17: A new **consumer-side schema drift** blinds the audit engine to its own most recent history. Commit
18: `248e967` ("stamp per-session model", epoch 1783408030) appended a fourth field to the transcript header
19: (`… · branch: <b> · model: <m>`), but the audit header regex (`internal/audit/parse.go:210`) is anchored
20: `…branch:\s*(\S+)\s*$`, so `parseHeader` rejects every post-stamp session as "malformed." **10 of 10
21: finalized sessions after the stamp are dropped; the window is empty only because of this.** This is the
22: same shape as session-audit-002's F1 collapse, moved to the consumer side and permanent (every future
23: session carries the tag). It also blocks this pass's own charter — re-measuring session-audit-003's
24: deferred long-turn residual against a clean post-fix baseline is impossible while the post-fix sessions
25: are unreadable.
26: 
27: - **F-A — parser rejects the `model:` header → `audit-parse-model-header` (P2, weight 64).** Dominant,
28:   gating: zeroes the mining mechanism for the whole post-stamp + future corpus. Fix: parse the header as
29:   a `·`-separated key:value list (require submodule/kind/branch, accept+ignore trailing extras, capture
30:   `model`). Not itself a token lever (stays P2), but the model field it captures is the input the ROI P1
31:   model-routing analysis needs.
32: - **F-B — a malformed-file spike is invisible to the loud alarm → `audit-malformed-visibility` (P2,
33:   weight 32).** `CorpusBroken`/`CorpusWarning` (aggregate.go:172-199) consult only stubs, never
34:   `ErrorCount()`; this pass the banner fired incidentally on 99 legacy stubs and blamed the wrong fix,
35:   and with those stubs gone the identical window would be byte-for-byte SILENT. The malformed-class
36:   analogue of the DONE `audit-stub-visibility`. Fix: fold `ErrorCount()` in with a distinct line steering
37:   to F-A.

beehive deterministic CLI

Usage:
  beehive [command]

Available Commands:
  audit       extract reproducible session metrics and select the next un-audited N-2 batch
  completion  Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
  help        Help about any command
  honeybee    honeybee process control
  hook        git hook management
  init        scaffold a beehive repo and install the git hooks (ROI-protect pre-commit + pre-receive, submodule-sync post-receive)
  instruction manage beehive instruction files (AGENTS.md, HONEYBEE.md, BOOTSTRAP.md)
  lint        validate the combined cross-submodule dependency graph is acyclic
  plan        manage PLAN.md
  secret      manage gpg-encrypted SECRETS.yaml.gpg
  submodule   manage beehive submodules
  task        manage PLAN.md tasks
  version     print version
  worktree    manage top-level beehive repo worktrees

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for beehive

Use "beehive [command] --help" for more information about a command.
